:mod:`json` --- Conversion to and from json
===========================================

.. module:: core.json
   :synopsis: Copnvertion to and from the json format

.. versionadded:: 0.1
.. moduleauthor:: Claude Gibert <claude.gibert@synopticview.co.uk>

The Json class
---------------------

.. class:: JSon(object)

    The **JSon** class only wraps the json (or simplejson) module's dump, dumps,
    load and loads functions. It ensures that:

    * floats are represented properly and there is no accuracy problem
      (see for example http://tinyurl.com/ykhqrre)
    * when decoding a json string, the standard module creates unicode 
      strings. These cannot be used for the name of variables before
      python 3.0, so at the moment, JSon convert all unicodes strings
      to python strs.

    .. classmethod:: encode(destination = None,\*\*kargs)
        
            :param destination: optional, if specified, a stream to encode into, if not, encoding is made in a string which is returned.
            :type destination: stream
            :param kargs: parameters to encode into json (encoded as a dictionary)
            :type kargs: names parameters

    .. classmethod:: decode(source)
    
            :param source: source to decode from
            :type source: either a stream (e.g. file) or a string

    Example::

        from semperpy.core.json import JSon

        s = JSon.encode(data={'hello':'world'},hello="world")
        print s
        {"data": {"hello": "world"}, "hello": "world"}
        print JSon.decode(s)
        {'data': {'hello': 'world'}, 'hello': 'world'}
